reasoning: Line 592 emits: echo esc_url( $this->get_url() ) . 'dist/meta-box.js?v=' . self::CSS_JS_VERSION. The esc_url() call wraps only $this->get_url(), which resolves to plugin_dir_url($this->file) — a server-controlled constant derived from the plugin's filesystem location. The appended suffix 'dist/meta-box.js?v=' is a string literal and self::CSS_JS_VERSION is a PHP class constant (const CSS_JS_VERSION = '3.4.1') — PHP class constants are not runtime-modifiable, cannot be filtered via apply_filters, and cannot be influenced by attacker-controlled input without source code modification. No component of the concatenated URL receives attacker-controlled data. The partial esc_url() coverage is a code smell and a fragile pattern, but it does not constitute a present vulnerability. The finding's own justification concedes the suffix is a compile-time constant, and the hypothetical about CSS_JS_VERSION becoming filterable is speculative, not evidence of a real flaw. Auth/reachability assessment (admin/editor) is accurate but irrelevant given no attacker-controlled sink exists.
